title: Hieracium bauhiniflorum
taxonomy: Hieracium bauhiniflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes over 1000 species of hawkweeds. The plant is further classified into the subfamily Carduoideae, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. Within the subfamily Carduoideae, Hieracium bauhiniflorum is part of the tribe Cardueae. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Hieracium bauhiniflorum is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly found in Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain and improving digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium bauhiniflorum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium bauhiniflorum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
